metadata.task_description: Cannot reset/delete selected items in division monitor - only fetch diff works, cannot reset selected category

findings.root_cause: DivisionMonitorWidget lacks integrated reset/delete functionality for category-selected items; reset capabilities exist only in separate MainWindow 'Reset/Delete DocID' tab

patterns.description: Sync button tooltip says 'mismatch rows stay for reset/delete' but no reset button exists in DivisionCard UI
patterns.evidence: sync_btn.setToolTip('Insert missing db_ptrj values only; mismatch rows stay for reset/delete')

patterns.description: _build_reset_docid_tab() uses config-based scope (Period, Division, Employee, Category) different from DivisionMonitor's per-division-card context
DivisionCard._on_run emits run_requested, _on_sync emits sync_requested, but NO reset_requested signal exists
patterns.evidence: run_requested = Signal(...) at line 473, sync_requested = Signal(...) at line 474, no reset_requested

integration_points.description: Sync button tooltip references reset but button does not exist - DivisionCard._category_card() needs reset button
DivisionCard signals only include run_requested and sync_requested, need reset_requested signal
_category_card() returns widget without reset button handler - needs _on_reset method
_build_reset_docid_tab() is separate reset mechanism using diff source, not integrated with DivisionMonitorWidget
run_reset_docid_delete() calls start_runner() with operation='delete_duplicates', used by separate tab

constraints: Reset functionality must work within DivisionMonitorWidget per-division per-category context, not config-based
Delete targets must come from compare-adtrans mismatch/extra status, not adtrans-doc-ids source
Session must be active before reset/delete (line 2489-2496 checks this)
Reset must handle MISS (extra in adjustments) and MISMATCH states differently

relevant_files.rationale: Primary UI file where reset/delete buttons should be added to DivisionCard - currently has run/sync but no reset for mismatched rows
relevant_files.topic_relation: Error-handling exploration targets this file because DivisionCard._category_card() (line 600) builds category widgets with run/sync buttons but no reset, causing inability to clear selected category items

relevant_files.rationale: Contains separate _build_reset_docid_tab() (line 1393) which has reset/delete logic but uses different scope/context from DivisionMonitorWidget
relevant_files.topic_relation: This file shows existing reset/delete pattern via run_reset_docid_delete() (line 2527) which could be reused for DivisionMonitor reset integration

relevant_files.rationale: DivisionRunDialog receives run signals from DivisionMonitorWidget via _on_division_monitor_run() (main_window.py:3499) - may need reset dialog variant
relevant_files.topic_relation: Shows how per-division-category dialog is structured; reset functionality would need similar dialog or integrate with existing structure

key_code.description: Builds category widget with Run and Sync Missing buttons but NO reset button - tooltip mentions reset (line 623) but button not implemented
Signals defined for run and sync actions but missing reset_requested signal for reset functionality
Creates separate reset/delete UI with fetch and run buttons - uses config scope, not DivisionMonitor context
Executes reset/delete via start_runner() with operation='delete_duplicates' - shows execution pattern to replicate for DivisionMonitor
Button click handlers emit signals; missing _on_reset() handler for new reset button

options: Should reset button clear MISMATCH rows only, or also EXTRA_IN_ADJUSTMENTS rows?
Should reset use browser automation (like existing reset_docid) or API-only delete?
Should reset work per-division per-category or batch across multiple divisions?
Is session check required before reset (like line 2489-2496 requires for reset_docid)?
clarification_needs.recommended: Add reset button to DivisionCard alongside Run/Sync Missing buttons, emit reset_requested signal, handle in MainWindow using existing delete_duplicates operation pattern from run_reset_docid_delete()
